reasoning: With the forecast date at December 7, 2025, only 24 days remain in the year. Although tensions are extremely high—marked by multiple US airstrikes on cartel-linked vessels (resulting in 60-80+ deaths) and a massive US naval deployment (10,000+ personnel) near Venezuela—the US has strictly confined these strikes to international waters/the EEZ, explicitly avoiding internationally recognized Venezuelan territory. While contingency plans for Tomahawk strikes on Venezuelan soil (e.g., ports) exist and troop deployments have not been ruled out, no finalized authorization has been reported. The probability remains buoyant because a single airstrike on VZ soil or an escalating exchange of fire between the US and Venezuelan navies (which are operating in close proximity) would trigger a YES. Given that the market was at 0.33 in late November, the passage of another 10 days without a resolving incident lowers the base probability slightly. I am placing the final estimate at 0.30, reflecting both the high threshold required by the location criteria and the fast-closing window of 2025 against the unprecedented level of military buildup.
